title: codex-cron bounded event-loop jobs for AO2 Pulse-style hardening
goal: Extend codex-cron with bounded zero-wait event-loop execution so a job can immediately continue based on a machine-readable decision emitted by its own output, while codex-cron remains scheduler/supervisor and AO2 Pulse remains planner/evidence protocol.
non_goals: Do not complete or replace the AO2 Pulse daemon.
Do not implement AO2-specific task selection inside codex-cron.
Do not remove existing interval, cron, once, tick, or daemon behavior.
Do not depend on real AO2 or Codex binaries in CI tests.

architecture.core_responsibility: Persist event-loop policy and parse event-loop decisions as pure logic.
architecture.cli_responsibility: Run a target job repeatedly with no sleep while the decision action is continue, bounded by max_chain_runs and max_runtime_seconds.
safety: Default to stop when no decision is emitted.
Stop on command failure, refused run, malformed decision, max chain, or max runtime.
Use the existing cross-process tick lock.
Use targeted job ticks so loop iterations do not fire unrelated due jobs.

acceptance: parse_event_loop_decision parses continue, stop, backoff, and fail.
Malformed schema-matching decision defaults to fail.
TickConfig supports an optional target_job_ids filter.
cmd_run uses targeted tick so running one job cannot trigger unrelated due jobs.
Core test proves only the selected due job fires.
codex-cron edit can enable, disable, and update event-loop policy.
codex-cron run-loop <id> immediately chains while action=continue.
run-loop prints status and iteration count.
codex-cron tick-loop expands due event-loop jobs into bounded zero-wait chains.
codex-cron daemon --event-loop uses tick-loop behavior between daemon intervals.
Existing codex-cron tick and daemon behavior remains unchanged when event-loop mode is not selected.
README documents event-loop jobs, decision schema, bounds, evidence path, tick-loop, and daemon --event-loop.
AO2 example shows codex-cron as scheduler/event-loop engine and AO2 Pulse as planner/evidence protocol.

handoff_notes: Use <repo>/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-12-codex-cron-event-loop.md as the detailed execution plan.
Preserve the existing uncommitted change in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-codex-cron-design.md.
Prefer TDD task-by-task implementation with frequent commits.
Do not implement AO2 Pulse one-shot in codex-cron; only document the expected integration contract.
